steps.title: Identify the unmovable conviction
steps.content: The signal is “no amount of justification...is likely to persuade.” This describes drivers with a fixed belief about the toll that arguments cannot change.

steps.title: Match to the blocking belief
steps.content:
Exorbitant (unreasonably high) describes a conviction that would block persuasion: if drivers believe the toll is already excessively high, no justification for raising it would change their minds.

Contentious, equivocal, and warranted don't capture a position that creates resistance to toll increases.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
